# stalwart_mailing_list (Resource)

Manages a Stalwart mailing list (the `MailingList` JMAP object).

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "stalwart_mailing_list" "announce" {
  domain_id   = stalwart_domain.example.id
  name        = "announce"
  description = "Company announcements"
  recipients = [
    "alice@example.com",
    "bob@example.com",
  ]
}

# Import an existing mailing list by its email address:
# terraform import stalwart_mailing_list.announce announce@example.com
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Mailing list name — the local part of the email address. Changing it replaces the mailing list.

### Optional

- `description` (String) Human-readable description of the mailing list.
- `domain` (String) Name of the domain this object belongs to, e.g. `example.com`. Resolved to a domain id at apply time. Mutually exclusive with `domain_id`.
- `domain_id` (String) Opaque id of the domain this object belongs to. Mutually exclusive with `domain`.
- `recipients` (Set of String) Email addresses that are members of the mailing list.

### Read-Only

- `email_address` (String) Full email address of the mailing list, formed as `name@domain` (server-set).
- `id` (String) Opaque server-assigned identifier of the mailing list.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import by email address (preferred):
terraform import stalwart_mailing_list.announce announce@example.com

# Import by opaque server-assigned id:
# terraform import stalwart_mailing_list.announce <opaque-id>
```
